g.data-narration: Welcome to clipping algorithms in computer graphics. We'll learn how graphics systems efficiently render visible content.
Clipping removes geometric parts outside the viewing area before rendering, improving efficiency and preventing display artifacts.
g.text: Process of keeping only portions of primitives inside the viewport/window.
Eliminates invisible content BEFORE rasterization â†’ saves computation

g.data-narration: Four main types: point clipping checks coordinates, line clipping handles segments, polygon clipping processes shapes, and text clipping handles fonts.
The window defines the world coordinate region, while viewport is the screen area. Clipping happens in normalized device coordinates.
g.text: Window â†’ NDC (clipping) â†’ Viewport transformation pipeline

g.data-narration: Cohen-Sutherland assigns 4-bit region codes to endpoints: Top=1000, Bottom=0100, Right=0010, Left=0001. Mnemonic TRBL.
The algorithm checks trivial acceptance first: both endpoints 0000 means accept. Then checks trivial rejection: AND non-zero means reject.
Example: Line from P1(100,50) to P2(200,150) clipped against window 120-180, 80-120. Let's compute the clipped result.
Liang-Barsky is a parametric algorithm using line equations x=x1+u*dx, y=y1+u*dy. More efficient than Cohen-Sutherland.
g.text: qâ‚پ = xâ‚پ-xmin, qâ‚‚ = xmax-xâ‚پ, qâ‚ƒ = yâ‚پ-ymin, qâ‚„ = ymax-yâ‚پ
For p<0: uâ‚پ = max(uâ‚پ, q/p), For p>0: uâ‚‚ = min(uâ‚‚, q/p)
Visible if uâ‚پ < uâ‚‚ and uâ‚‚ â‰¥ 0 and uâ‚پ â‰¤ 1

g.data-narration: Example: Clip line (10,30) to (50,50) against window (20,20)-(40,40). Computing p, q values and u parameters.
Sutherland-Hodgman clips polygon against one window edge at a time. For each edge, keep inside vertices, add intersections for crossing edges.
Point clipping simply checks if coordinates are within bounds. Text clipping has three methods: all-or-nothing, per-character, and pixel-level.
g.text: xmin â‰¤ x â‰¤ xmax AND ymin â‰¤ y â‰¤ ymax

g.data-narration: Cohen-Sutherland is easier to understand; Liang-Barsky is more efficient for lines. Sutherland-Hodgman is standard for polygon clipping.
Remember: clipping eliminates invisible content before rendering. Region codes enable quick acceptance or rejection checks. Parametric methods are more efficient.
g.text: Clipping happens BEFORE rasterization to save computation
Cohen-Sutherland: 4-bit region codes (TRBL), trivial accept/reject first
Liang-Barsky: Parametric u [0,1], more efficient than Cohen-Sutherland
Sutherland-Hodgman: Process one window edge at a time
